title: An exploratory open-label randomized trial comparing Risperidone long-Acting Injectable with oral antipsychotic medication in the treatment of early psychosis
notes: RCT (randomized); schizophrenia spectrum (DSM); risperidone LAI vs oral SGAs (pharma); Canadian (Malla, Chue, Stip - Canadian PIs); 2016; English. Meets all criteria.

title: Dopamine D2/3 Receptor Occupancy Following Dose Reduction Is Predictable With Minimal Plasma Antipsychotic Concentrations
notes: Not a traditional RCT - open-label dose reduction PK/PD study measuring receptor occupancy. No randomized comparison groups for clinical outcomes. This is a pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic study, not a clinical efficacy RCT. Fails criterion 1.

title: The effects of desvenlafaxine on neurocognitive and work functioning in employed outpatients with major depressive disorder.
notes: Open-label single-arm study (n=36) of desvenlafaxine. No randomization, no control group. Not an RCT. Fails criterion 1.

title: The Time Course of Effect of Multilayer-Release Methylphenidate Hydrochloride Capsules: A Randomized Double-Blind Study of Adults With ADHD
notes: RCT; ADHD (DSM); methylphenidate (pharma); however authors are Wigal SB, Wigal T, Childress A, Donnelly GAE, Reiz JL - these are US-based investigators. No Canadian site or PI evident. Published 2020. Fails criterion 4 (non-Canadian).

title: Effects of varenicline versus transdermal nicotine replacement therapy on cigarette demand on quit day in individuals with substance use disorders
notes: RCT; substance use disorders; varenicline vs NRT (pharma); but authors Murphy CM, MacKillop J, Martin RA, Tidey JW, Colby SM, Rohsenow DJ - US-based researchers at Brown University. MacKillop later moved to McMaster but this study is US-based. Also, smoking cessation pharma - the MH condition is SUD but the target of treatment is smoking, not the SUD mental health condition per se. Fails criterion 4 (non-Canadian).

title: The THINC-it Tool for Cognitive Assessment and Measurement in Major Depressive Disorder: Sensitivity to Change.
notes: Open-label single-arm study of vortioxetine to validate THINC-it cognitive tool. No randomization, no control group. This is a tool validation study, not an RCT. Fails criterion 1.

title: Efficacy and safety of balovaptan for socialisation and communication difficulties in autistic adults in North America and Europe
notes: Phase 3 RCT; ASD (DSM-5); balovaptan vs placebo (pharma); Canada is one of 6 countries with sites, Anagnostou E (Canadian PI); 2022; English. ASD is a DSM-5 diagnosis. Meets all criteria.

title: Head-To-Head Comparison of Vortioxetine Versus Desvenlafaxine in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder With Partial Response to SSRI Therapy: Results of the VIVRE Study.
notes: RCT (randomized double-blind active-controlled); MDD (DSM-5); vortioxetine vs desvenlafaxine (pharma); Canadian PI (McIntyre RS, University of Toronto); 2023; English. Main results of VIVRE study. Meets all criteria.

title: Combined Modality Intervention for ADHD With Comorbid Reading Disorders: A Proof of Concept Study.
notes: RCT; ADHD+reading disorder (DSM); methylphenidate vs placebo (pharma); Canadian (Tannock, Ickowicz - SickKids/Toronto); 2018; English. Meets all criteria.

title: Investigating the safety and efficacy of nabilone for the treatment of agitation in patients with moderate-to-severe Alzheimer's disease.
notes: This is a STUDY PROTOCOL, not main results. Abstract describes the design of a cross-over RCT but does not report results. Fails exclusion criterion (protocol without results).

title: Cost-effectiveness analysis of a randomized study of depression treatment options in primary care suggests stepped-care treatment may have economic benefits.
notes: This is a cost-effectiveness/economic analysis of a previously published RCT. The stepped-care pathway includes non-pharmacological treatments (online CBT, TAU). The primary intervention is a care pathway model, not a specific pharmacotherapy. Also a secondary economic analysis. Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma primary intervention) and exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: The effect of intravenous ketamine on cognitive functions in adults with treatment-resistant major depressive or bipolar disorders.
notes: Retrospective study from the Canadian Rapid Treatment Center of Excellence. No randomization - this is a retrospective analysis of patients who received IV ketamine in clinical practice (NCT04209296 is a registry). Not an RCT. Fails criterion 1.

title: D-cycloserine normalizes long-term motor plasticity after transcranial magnetic intermittent theta-burst stimulation in major depressive disorder.
notes: Randomized crossover study using D-cycloserine + TMS to measure motor evoked potentials (motor cortex plasticity). n=12 MDD, n=12 healthy. The primary intervention is neuromodulation (TMS/iTBS) and D-cycloserine is used to test NMDA receptor involvement in motor plasticity. The outcome is motor cortex plasticity (MEPs), not MH symptom improvement. This is a mechanistic neuroscience study, not a pharmacotherapy RCT targeting MH outcomes. Fails criterion 3.

title: Safety and efficacy of laquinimod for Huntington's disease (LEGATO-HD).
notes: RCT; laquinimod vs placebo; Canada is one of 10 countries; 2024; English. However, Huntington's disease is a neurodegenerative movement disorder, not a DSM-5 mental health diagnosis. The primary outcome is motor function (UHDRS-TMS) and caudate volume. Fails criterion 2 (not a diagnosed MH condition per DSM-5).

title: Slowing Cognitive Decline in Major Depressive Disorder and Mild Cognitive Impairment: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
notes: RCT; MDD/MCI; Canadian (5 hospitals in Toronto); 2025; English. However, the intervention is cognitive remediation (CR) plus transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) - neither is pharmacotherapy. CR is a behavioral intervention and tDCS is neuromodulation. Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma).

title: The Influence of Posttraumatic Stress Disorder on Treatment Outcomes of Patients With Borderline Personality Disorder
notes: Secondary analysis of an RCT comparing DBT vs GPM for BPD. DBT and GPM are psychotherapies, not pharmacotherapy. Also a secondary analysis examining moderator effects of PTSD on outcomes. Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma) and exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: A patient decision aid for antidepressant use in pregnancy: study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
notes: This is explicitly a STUDY PROTOCOL. Title states 'study protocol for a randomized controlled trial.' No results reported. Fails exclusion criterion (protocol without results).

title: Visual working memory deterioration preceding relapse in psychosis
notes: RCT of quetiapine continuation vs placebo discontinuation in first-episode psychosis. However, authors are Hui CLM, Li YK, etc. from Hong Kong (University of Hong Kong). No Canadian site or PI. Fails criterion 4 (non-Canadian).

title: Effect of Aptensio XR (Methylphenidate HCl Extended-Release) Capsules on Sleep in Children with ADHD
notes: Two studies of MPH-MLR for ADHD. Authors are Owens J, Weiss M, Nordbrock E, etc. Weiss M is a Canadian PI (UBC). RCT; ADHD (DSM); methylphenidate (pharma); Canadian PI; 2016; English. However, the primary outcome is SLEEP measures (exploratory endpoints from pivotal studies). This is a secondary/exploratory analysis of sleep data from two pivotal RCTs, not the main results publication. Fails exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: Clinical meaningfulness of ADAS-Cog subscale change in relation to goal attainment in patients on cholinesterase inhibitors
notes: Exploratory post-hoc analysis comparing ADAS-Cog changes with Goal Attainment Scaling in patients on donepezil or galantamine. This is a secondary/exploratory analysis of previously published RCT data, not a main results publication. Rockwood K is Canadian. Fails exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: Serum and plasma BDNF and response in a randomized controlled trial of riluzole for treatment resistant depression
notes: Biomarker (BDNF) analysis from an RCT of riluzole for TRD. Authors are Wilkinson ST, Kiselycznyk C, Banasr M, etc. - US-based (Yale, Baylor). Secondary biomarker analysis, not main results. Also non-Canadian. Fails criterion 4 (non-Canadian) and exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: Feasibility and efficacy of a hospital-based violence intervention program on reducing repeat violent injury in youth
notes: RCT of an Emergency Department Violence Intervention Program. This is a behavioral/psychosocial intervention, not pharmacotherapy. Also, the target is violence-related injury, not a diagnosed MH condition. Fails criteria 2 and 3.

title: Rationale and design of an international randomized placebo-controlled trial of a 36-ingredient micronutrient supplement for children with ADHD: The MADDY study
notes: This is a STUDY PROTOCOL ('Rationale and design'). No results reported. Fails exclusion criterion (protocol without results).

title: Efficacy and cost-effectiveness of intensive short-term dynamic psychotherapy for treatment resistant depression: Halifax depression trial
notes: RCT of Intensive Short-Term Dynamic Psychotherapy (ISTDP) vs community mental health for TRD. ISTDP is a psychotherapy, not pharmacotherapy. Canadian (Halifax). Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma).

title: Neural effects of oxytocin and mimicry in frontotemporal dementia: A randomized crossover study
notes: Randomized crossover of intranasal oxytocin in FTD. Canadian (Finger EC at Western). However, the outcome is fMRI BOLD signal (brain imaging), not clinical MH outcomes. This is a mechanistic neuroimaging study, not a clinical efficacy trial reporting main clinical results. N=28. Secondary/mechanistic analysis. Fails exclusion criterion (not main clinical results).

title: The Impact of a Harry Potter-Based CBT Skills Curriculum on Suicidality and Well-being in Middle Schoolers
notes: RCT of a CBT-based mental health literacy curriculum delivered by teachers. This is a psychoeducational/psychosocial intervention, not pharmacotherapy. Canadian (Toronto). Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma).

title: Treatment of mixed depression with theta-burst stimulation: results from a double-blind randomized sham-controlled clinical trial
notes: RCT of theta-burst stimulation (TBS) for mixed depression. TBS is a form of transcranial magnetic stimulation (neuromodulation), not pharmacotherapy. Authors are from Brazil. Fails criterion 3 (non-pharma) and criterion 4 (non-Canadian).

title: The safety and efficacy of fecal microbiota transplantation in bipolar disorder during depressive episodes: study protocol for a pilot RCT
notes: This is a STUDY PROTOCOL ('study protocol for a pilot randomized controlled trial'). No results reported. Canadian (Taylor VH, Toronto). Fails exclusion criterion (protocol without results).

title: Building Emotional Awareness and Mental Health (BEAM): A Pilot RCT of an App-Based Program for Mothers of Toddlers
notes: Pilot RCT of an app-based psychoeducation program (BEAM) for mothers. This is a digital/psychosocial intervention, not pharmacotherapy. Participants are mothers (not diagnosed MH population). Fails criteria 2 and 3.

title: A cross-cultural randomized pilot trial of western-based and five elements music therapy for psychological well-being
notes: Pilot RCT of music therapy interventions. Music therapy is not pharmacotherapy. N=35 total (23 from Canada, 12 from China). Participants selected for stress, not diagnosed MH conditions. Fails criteria 2 and 3.

title: An investigation of cortical thickness and antidepressant response in major depressive disorder: A CAN-BIND study report.
notes: CAN-BIND study - this is a neuroimaging biomarker analysis examining whether cortical thickness predicts antidepressant response. While it comes from an RCT of escitalopram (then aripiprazole add-on), this paper reports neuroimaging predictors of response, not the main clinical results. The main CAN-BIND results were published separately. Secondary/biomarker analysis. Fails exclusion criterion (secondary analysis).

title: A double-blind placebo-controlled trial of minocycline on translocator protein distribution volume in treatment-resistant major depressive disorder.
notes: Double-blind placebo-controlled trial of minocycline in TRD. Canadian (Meyer JH, CAMH Toronto). However, n=21, and the primary outcome is TSPO VT (a PET neuroimaging biomarker of gliosis), not clinical MH outcomes. This is a mechanistic PET imaging study, not a clinical efficacy RCT reporting main clinical results. Fails criterion 5 (not main clinical results) - this is a biomarker/mechanistic study.

title: Intranasal oxytocin for apathy in people with frontotemporal dementia (FOXY)
category: pharmacological RCT for FTD apathy in Canada
notes: Phase 2a/2b RCT; FTD with apathy (DSM-5 neurocognitive disorder); intranasal oxytocin vs placebo (pharma); Canadian sites and PIs (Hsiung at UBC, Laforce, Bherer - multicentre Canadian-led); 2025; English. Meets all criteria.

title: Safety and Efficacy of Varenicline for Smoking Cessation in Alcohol-Dependent Smokers in Concurrent Treatment for Alcohol Use Disorder.
notes: Pilot RCT (randomized double-blind placebo-controlled); tobacco use disorder in comorbid alcohol dependence (both DSM-5 diagnoses); varenicline vs placebo (pharma targeting tobacco dependence); Canadian (Zawertailo, Le Foll, Selby - CAMH/University of Toronto); 2020; English. Meets all criteria.
